summary: Senior Data Engineer with 14+ years of experience building scalable ETL/ELT pipelines, Medallion architectures, and HIPAA-compliant healthcare data systems. Strong expertise in SQL optimization, SSIS, DataStage, Databricks, Microsoft Fabric, and near real-time data processing. Recent hands-on experience with LLMs, RAG systems, prompt engineering, and AI workflow optimization.
core_strengths: Designing large-scale data pipelines (batch and near real-time)
Healthcare domain expertise (HIPAA, X12, ICD-10, CMS, FACETS)
LLM application development (RAG, prompt engineering, evals)

evidence_stories.headline: Built production-grade RAG chatbot from scratch
evidence_stories.summary: Built a production-grade RAG chatbot POC with ingestion, chunking, embeddings, indexing, and full prompt pipeline for domain-grounded Q&A with source attribution.

evidence_stories.headline: Reduced LLM prompt size 98%, cut latency 40%
evidence_stories.summary: Applied instruction fine-tuning to compress LLM prompts from 1800 to 30 characters, reducing model latency (TTFT) by ~40% and drastically shrinking token footprint at scale.

evidence_stories.headline: Schema-agnostic ETL cut onboarding effort by 90%
evidence_stories.summary: Designed a schema-agnostic ETL pipeline for flat file to DB ingestion. New file formats could be onboarded without code changes, eliminating manual engineering intervention.

evidence_stories.headline: 1M+ EDI X12 transactions/month, high availability
evidence_stories.summary: Maintained a near-real-time, high-availability healthcare pipeline processing over 1M EDI X12 transactions monthly under strict HIPAA constraints.

evidence_stories.headline: 400GB state-level healthcare ingestion pipeline
evidence_stories.summary: Built SSIS pipeline ingesting 400GB of state-level healthcare datasets into a structured warehouse with cleansing, SCD logic, and conformed dimensions in Microsoft Fabric.

evidence_stories.headline: Re-engineered enrollment system; docs became system blueprint
evidence_stories.summary: Led a team of 10 to re-engineer the enrollment system from .Net to Sybase. Authored framework documentation that was later used as the blueprint for a full system rewrite.
